Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 1, 016 [Erra and Išum 1][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
Column i
o i 1o i 1

⸢LUGAL⸣ gi-mir da-ad₂-me ba-nu-u ⸢kib⸣-[ra-a-ti ...]

(o i 1) O king of the whole inhabited world, creator of the (four) edges, [...],

o i 22

dḪENDUR.SAG.GA₂ IBILA dEN.LIL₂ reš-⸢tu⸣-[u ...]

(o i 2) o Hendursag,1 first-born son of Ellil, [...],

o i 33

na-aš₂ ḫaṭ-ṭu ṣir-ti na-qid ṣal-mat ⸢SAG⸣.[DU ...]

(o i 3) bearer of the exalted sceptre, herdsman of the black-headed people, [...]

o i 44

di-šum ṭa₂-bi-ḫu na-aʾ-du ša₂ ana ⸢na⸣-[še-e ...]

(o i 4) o Išum,2 praised slaughterer, who [...] to carry [...]

o i 55

ana šub-ruq ul-me-šu₂ še-ru-ti dER₃.RA ⸢qar⸣-[rad DINGIR-MEŠ ...]

(o i 5) (and) to make his fierce axes shine! (While) Erra, hero [of the gods, ...],

o i 66

i-ris-su-ma lib₃-ba-šu₂ e-peš [...]

(o i 6) his (Išum's) heart wanted him to do [...].

o i 77

i-ta-mi a-na gišTUKUL-MEŠ-šu₂ lit-[pa-ta ...]

(o i 7) He (Išum) spoke to his weapons: "Anoint yourselves [...]!"

o i 88

ana d7.BI qar-rad la ša₂-na-an na-⸢an⸣-[di-qa ...]

(o i 8) To the Seven, unrivalled heroes, (he said): "Put on [...]!"

o i 99

i-qab-bi-ma a-na ka-ša₂ lu-[ṣi-ma ...]

(o i 9) And he said to you (Erra): "I wish to go out [...]

o i 1010

at-ta di-pa-ru-um-ma i-na-⸢ṭa⸣-[lu ...]

(o i 10) "You are the torch, and they will see [...],

o i 1111

at-ta a-lik maḫ-ri-im-ma [...]

(o i 11) "You are the advance guard and [...],

o i 1212

at-ta nam-ṣa-ru-um-ma ṭa-bi-⸢ḫu⸣ [...]

(o i 12) "You are the sword and the [...] slaughterer.

o i 1313

dER₃.RA ti-bi-ma ⸢i⸣-[na ...]

(o i 13) "Erra, stand up from [...]!

o i 1414

⸢ki⸣-i nam-rat kab-ta-⸢ta? ḫa-du⸣-u₂ ⸢lib⸣-[bu-uk]

(o i 14) "How bright (your) mood (will be), how joyful your heart!"

o i 1515

[d]⸢ER₃⸣.RA ki-i ša₂ a-mi-⸢li dal-pi⸣ i-da-a-šu₂ [...]

(o i 15) Erra, whose arms [...] like those of a sleepless man,

o i 1616

[i-qab]-⸢bi⸣ a-na lib₃-bi-⸢šu₂⸣ lu-ut-⸢bi⸣ lu-uṣ-[lal-ma]

(o i 16) said to his heart: "Should I stand up (or) lie down?"

o i 1717

⸢i-ta⸣-[a-ma a-na gišTUKUL]-⸢MEŠ⸣-šu₂ um-me-da tub₂-qa-⸢a⸣-[ti]

(o i 17) And he said [to] his [weapons]: "Hide in the corners!"

o i 1818

[ana] d7.⸢BI⸣ [qar-rad la ša₂]-na-an ana šub-ti-ku-nu ⸢tu⸣-ra-⸢ma⸣

(o i 18) [(And) to] the Seven, unrivalled [heroes]: "Return to your dwellings!"

o i 1919

[a]-di at-ta ⸢ta⸣-[de]-⸢ek⸣-ku-šu₂ ṣa-lil ur-šu-uš-šu₂

(o i 19) Until you (Išum) wake him up, he will be asleep in his bedroom,

o i 2020

⸢it⸣-ti dma-ma ⸢ḫi-ra⸣-tuš ip-pu-šu₂ ul-⸢ṣa⸣-am-ma

(o i 20) enjoying himself with Mami, his wife.

o i 2121

[dEN].GI₆.DU.DU EN mut-⸢tal-lik GE₆⸣ mut-tar-ru-u₂ NUN-MEŠ

(o i 21) He is Engidudu,3 "the lord who roams at night," "the guide of the nobles,"

o i 2222

[ša₂] GURUŠ u munusKI.SIKIL ina ⸢šul-mu it-ta⸣-nar-ru-u₂ u₂-⸢nam⸣-ma-ru GIM u₄-mi

(o i 22) [who] always leads a boy and a girl into safety, making (them) shine like the day.

o i 2323

[ša₂ d]7.BI qar-rad la ša₂-na-an šu-⸢un-na⸣-ta DINGIR-su-un

(o i 23) The divinity [of] the Seven, unrivalled heroes, is very different:

o i 2424

[i]-lit-ta-šu₂-nu a-ḫat-ma ma-lu-u ⸢pul⸣-ḫa-a-ti

(o i 24) their origin is strange and they are full of fearsomeness,

o i 2525

⸢a⸣-mir-šu₂-nu uš-taḫ-ḫaṭ-ma na-pis-su-nu ⸢mu⸣-tum₃-ma

(o i 25) Whoever sees them is intimidated and their breath is death.

o i 2626

⸢UN-MEŠ⸣ šaḫ-tu-ma ul iʾ-mi-ri [ana] ⸢ša₂⸣-a-šu

(o i 26) People are afraid and do not approach it (their breath).

o i 2727

⸢d⸣[i]-šum giš⸢IG⸣-ma e-dil pa-nu-[uš-šu₂]-⸢un⸣

(o i 27) Išum is the door, shut in front of them.

o i 2828

da-⸢nu⸣-um LUGAL DINGIR-MEŠ KI⸢ti₃ ir⸣-[ḫe]-⸢e-ma⸣

(o i 28) Anu, king of the gods, inseminated the earth, and

o i 2929

7 DINGIR-⸢MEŠ⸣ ul-da-aš₂-šum-ma d7.BI it-ta-bi [zi]-kir-šu₂-un

(o i 29) she bore him 7 gods and he named them the Seven.

o i 3030

iz-za-⸢az⸣-zu ina maḫ-ri-šu-ma ši-mat-su-nu ⸢i-šim⸣-ma

(o i 30) They stood before him and he determined their destinies.

o i 3131

⸢is⸣-si-⸢ma⸣ 1en i-šak-ka-na ⸢ṭe₃⸣-e-mu

(o i 31) He summoned the first to give (him) orders:

o i 3232

[e]-ma [ta]-⸢na⸣-di-ru-ma ta!(I-)tal-ku ma-ḫi-ra ⸢e tar⸣-ši

(o i 32) "Wherever you go off to rage, you shall not have an opponent."

o i 3333

i-⸢qab⸣-[bi ana] 2i GIM ⸢dGIRA₂⸣ ku-bu-um-ma ḫu-muṭ ⸢GIM nab⸣-li

(o i 33) He said to the second: "Scorch like fire and burn like a flame!"

o i 3434

i-⸢ta⸣-[mi] ⸢ana⸣ šal-ši ⸢zi⸣-im la-bi lu šak-na-⸢ta⸣-ma a-mir-ka ⸢liš⸣-ḫar-miṭ

(o i 34) He spoke to the third: "May you be provided with the appearance of a lion, so that whoever sees you dissolves."

o i 3535

i-qab-[bi ana] re-bi-i a-na na-še-e gišTUKUL-MEŠ-⸢ka⸣ ez-⸢zu⸣-ti KURu₂ ⸢li-tab⸣-bit

(o i 35) He said to the fourth: "May a mountain be destroyed completely at the lifting of your furious weapons."

o i 3636

a-na [5]⸢ši⸣ iq-ta-bi GIM TU₁₅ ⸢ziq-ma⸣ kip-pat ḫi-iṭ-⸢ṭa⸣

(o i 36) To the fifth he said: "Blow like the wind and scour the circumference (of the earth)!"

o i 3737

⸢šeš⸣-[ša₂] ⸢um⸣-ta-ʾi-ir e-liš ⸢u⸣ šap-liš ba-aʾ-ma la ta-gam-mil mam-ma

(o i 37) The sixth he instructed: "Go along above and below and spare nobody!"

o i 3838

ša-⸢ba⸣-a i-mat ba-aš₂-mi i-⸢ṣe⸣-en-šum-ma u₂-šam-qat ZI⸢ti₃⸣

(o i 38) The seventh he filled up with the venom of a poisonous snake so that he could wreck lives.

o i 3939

ul-tu ⸢ši⸣-mat d7.BI ⸢nap⸣-ḫar-šu₂-nu i-ši-mu da-nu₃

(o i 39) After Anu had determined the destinies of all of the Seven,

o i 4040

id-din-šu-⸢nu⸣-ti-ma ana ⸢d⸣ER₃.RA qar-rad DINGIR-MEŠ lil-li-ku i-da-a-šu

(o i 40) he gave them to Erra, hero of the gods, that they may go at his side,

o i 4141

ki-i ša₂ UN-⸢MEŠ da-ad₂⸣-me ḫu-bur-ši-na UGU-ka im-tar-ṣu

(o i 41) (saying) "When the bustle of the people of the inhabited world displeases you,

o i 4242

ub-lam-ma ⸢lib₃⸣-ba-ka a-na ša₂-kan ka-ma-ri

(o i 42) "and your heart desires to accomplish defeat,

o i 4343

ṣal-mat SAG.DU a-⸢na⸣ šu-mut-ti šum-qu-tu₄ bu-li₃ dŠAKKAN₂

(o i 43) "to kill the black-headed people (and) to wreck the livestock of Sumuqan,

o i 4444

lu-u₂ gišTUKUL-MEŠ-⸢ka ez⸣-zu-ti šu-nu-ma lil-li-ku!(LU) i-da-a-ka

(o i 44) "may they be your furious weapons and may they go at your side."

o i 4545

šu-nu ez-zu-⸢ma⸣ ti-bu-u₂ kak-ki!(KU-)šu₂-un

(o i 45) (Now) they (the Seven) are furious and are raising their weapons.

o i 4646

i-ta-mu-u₂ a-⸢na⸣ dER₃.RA ti-bi i-zi-iz-ma

(o i 46) They talk to Erra: "Get up and stand!

o i 4747

min₃-su ki-i ši-⸢i⸣-bi muq-qi₂ tu-šib ina IRI

(o i 47) "Why do you sit in the city like a weakened old man?

o i 4848

ki-i šer₃-ri la-⸢ʾi⸣-i tu-šib ina E₂

(o i 48) "(Why) do you sit at home like a young child, a baby?

o i 4949

ki-i <la> a-lik EDIN ⸢ni⸣-ka-la a-kal sin-niš

(o i 49) "Should we eat women's food like soldiers who do not go to the open country?

o i 5050

ki-i ša₂ ta-ḫa-zi ⸢la⸣ ni-du-u₂ ni-ip-la-ḫa ni-ru-da

(o i 50) "Should we fear and tremble as if we do not know battle?

o i 5151

a-lak EDIN ša₂ eṭ-lu-⸢ti⸣ ki-i ša₂ i-sin-nu-um-ma

(o i 51) "Going to open country is like a festival for young men!

o i 5252

a-šib IRI NUNu₂ ul i-šeb-bi ak-la

(o i 52) "The city-dweller, the noble, does not become sated with food.

o i 5353

šum-suk ina KA UN-MEŠ-šu₂-ma qa-lil SAG.DU-su

(o i 53) "In the opinion of his people, he will be rejected and belittled

o i 5454

a-na a-lik EDIN a-ki-i i-tar-ra-aṣ qa-as-su

(o i 54) "as soon as he stretches out his hand against a soldier who does go to the open country.

o i 5555

ša₂ a-šib IRI lu pu-ug-gu-lat ku!(LU-)bu-uk-ku-[uš]

(o i 55) "(Because) although the strength of a city-dweller can be very great,

o i 5656

a-na a-lik EDIN a-ki-i i-dan-nin ⸢mi-i-na⸣

(o i 56) "how can he be stronger than a soldier who does go to the open country?

o i 5757

a-kal IRI lul-lu-u₂ ul ub-ba-la ka-⸢man tum₃⸣-[ri]

(o i 57) "Luxurious city-bread is not worth (as much as) a charcoal-baked loaf.

o i 5858

ši-kar na-aš₂-pi du-uš-šu-pi ul ub-ba-lu A-⸢MEŠ na⸣-a-[di]

(o i 58) "Thin, sweetened beer is not worth (as much as) water from a waterskin.

o i 5959

E₂.GAL tam-li-i ul ub-ba-la ma-ṣal-la-tu ša₂ [...]

(o i 59) "A terraced palace is not worth (as much as) the shelters of [...].

o i 6060

qu-ra-du dER₃.RA ṣi-i-ma ana ⸢EDIN⸣ tu-ruk [giš]TUKUL-MEŠ-⸢ka⸣

(o i 60) "Warrior Erra, go out to the open country and beat your weapons!

o i 6161

ri-gim-ka dun-nin-ma liš-tar-i-⸢bu⸣ e-liš u šap-⸢liš⸣

(o i 61) "Make your voice strong so that above and below shall shake!

o i 6262

di₂-gi₃-gi₃ liš-mu-ma li-šar-bu-[u] MU-⸢ka⸣

(o i 62) "May the Igigi hear (it) and make your name great!

o i 6363

dA.NUN.NA.KI liš-mu-ma liš-ḫu-⸢ṭu⸣ zi-⸢kir⸣-ka

(o i 63) "May the Annunaku hear (it) and fear your name!

o i 6464

DINGIR-MEŠ liš-mu-ma lik-nu-⸢šu⸣ ana ni-ri-ka

(o i 64) "May the gods hear (it) and submit to your yoke!

o i 6565

ma-al-ki liš-mu-ma lik-mi-⸢su⸣ ša₂-⸢pal⸣-ka

(o i 65) "May the rulers hear (it) and kneel before you!

o i 6666

⸢KUR⸣.KUR liš-ma-ma bi-lat-si-⸢na⸣ [liš]-⸢ša₂-a⸣-ka

(o i 66) "May the lands hear (it) and bring you their tribute!

o i 6767

⸢qal⸣-lu-u₂ liš-mu-ma ina ra-⸢ma⸣-[ni-šu₂-nu li-mu]-⸢tu⸣

(o i 67) "May the unimportant men hear (it) and die by their own (hand)!

o i 6868

⸢dan⸣-nu liš-mi-ma liš-šu-⸢ur⸣ [e-mu-qi₂]-šu₂

(o i 68) "May the powerful man hear (it) and may his strength diminish!

Column ii
o ii 1o ii 1

[... zaq]-⸢ru-te liš-mu⸣-[ma ...]

(o ii 1) "May the high [...] hear (it) [and ...]!

o ii 22

[... gal]-⸢la⸣-a-tu₂ liš-ma-⸢ma⸣ [...]

(o ii 2) "May the rolling [...] hear (it) and [...]!

o ii 33

⸢ša⸣ qi₂-i-⸢ši⸣ [dan]-ni lik-⸢tap⸣-pi-[ru ...]

(o ii 3) "May [...] of the mighty forest be cleared away!

o ii 44

⸢a⸣-pu ša₂ ne₂-⸢re⸣-<ba> ⸢la⸣ i-šu-u₂ li-⸢iḫ⸣-[ta-aṣ-ṣi-ṣu ...]

(o ii 4) "May the [...] of the reed-beds which have no entrance be snapped off!

o ii 55

⸢UN⸣-MEŠ lip-⸢la⸣-ḫa-ma lit-qu-[na ...]

(o ii 5) "May the people be afraid and calm [...]!

o ii 66

⸢bu⸣-lu₄ li-ru-ur-ma li-tur [...]

(o ii 6) "May the livestock tremble and turn [...]!

o ii 77

DINGIR-MEŠ AD-MEŠ-⸢ka⸣ li-mu-ru-ma li-na-⸢du⸣ [...]

(o ii 7) "May the gods, your forefathers, see and praise [...]!

o ii 88

⸢qu⸣-ra-du dER₃.⸢RA⸣ min₃-su EDIN ⸢tu⸣-<maš>-⸢šir₃⸣-ma ⸢tu⸣-[šib ina IRI]

(o ii 8) "Warrior Erra, why did you abandon the open country to sit [in the city]?

o ii 99

bu-ul dŠAKKAN₂ ⸢u₃ nam⸣-maš-še-e le-qu-u [ši-ṭu-tu₄]

(o ii 9) "The livestock of Sumuqan and the animals scorn (us).

o ii 1010

qu-ra-du dER₃.⸢RA ni⸣-qab-bi-kum₂-ma at-mu-ni [...]

(o ii 10) "Warrior Erra, we address you and our speech [...].

o ii 1111

[a]-⸢di⸣ ma-a-⸢tu⸣ [nap]-⸢ḫar⸣-ša₂ ir-bu-u₂ [...]

(o ii 11) "Until all the land becomes greater [...],

o ii 1212

[min₃]-⸢de⸣-[ma at]-⸢ta?⸣ ši-ma-a-ti [...]

(o ii 12) "you will certainly hear [...].

o ii 1313

[... ra-aʾ]-⸢im⸣ šaḫ-ra-ar₂-ti SIG₅ti [...]

(o ii 13) "[...], who love deathly hush, [...] good:

o ii 1414

[... ḫu]-⸢bur⸣ UN-MEŠ ul i-re-eḫ-ḫu-u₂ [...]

(o ii 14) "[...] cannot flow over [...] the bustle of the people.

o ii 1515

[... gi]-pa-ra ra-ḫi-iṣ ⸢MAŠ₂.ANŠE⸣

(o ii 15) "Livestock is trampling [...], the pastureland.

o ii 1616

[...] x-u-šu₂ i-bak-ki ⸢ṣar⸣-piš

(o ii 16) "[...] weeps loud and bitterly [...] his [...].

o ii 1717

⸢ne₂⸣-[e-šu ... u₂]-⸢šam⸣-qa-tu MAŠ₂.ANŠE ⸢d⸣ŠAKKAN₂

(o ii 17) "Lion [...] wreck the livestock of Sumuqan.

o ii 1818

⸢re⸣-[ʾu-u ...] GE₆ ul i-ṣal-lal ⸢i-bal-ka ka⸣-a-ša₂

(o ii 18) "The shepherd cannot sleep [...] at night [...]; he pleads with you.

o ii 1919

⸢u₃⸣ [... KUR]⸢i⸣ nim-ta-⸢aš₂⸣-[ši?] ⸢ḫar⸣-ra-nu

(o ii 19) "Moreover, [...] of the mountains, we have forgotten the way.

o ii 2020

[...]-⸢it⸣ qe₂-⸢e et-tu⸣-tu

(o ii 20) "[...] ... a spider's web.

o ii 2121

[... ib-bal-kit]-⸢ma id-ni-na UGU e-mu-qi₂⸣-ni

(o ii 21) "[...] resists and is stronger than our strength.

o ii 2222

[...] li-ša₂-⸢an⸣-šu₂

(o ii 22) "[...] its blade [...].

o ii 2323

pa-tar-⸢ni⸣ [... it-ta]-⸢di⸣ šu-uḫ-tu₂

(o ii 23) "Our sword [...] has lain down rust."

o ii 2424

iš-me-⸢šu⸣-nu-⸢ti⸣-[ma] ⸢qu-ra⸣-du dER₃.RA

(o ii 24) The warrior Erra listened to them and

o ii 2525

a-mat d⸢7⸣.BI iq-bu-u₂ ki-i u₂-lu ⸢I₃.GIŠ⸣ UGU-šu₂ ⸢i⸣-ṭib

(o ii 25) the word the Seven said was pleasing to him like the best oil.

o ii 2626

i-pu-⸢uš⸣-ma pa-a-šu i-zak-kar ana d[i]-⸢šum⸣

(o ii 26) He opened his mouth to speak to Išum:

o ii 2727

min₃-su še-ma-ta-ma qa-liš tu-⸢uš-bu⸣

(o ii 27) "Why, (though) you are hearing (this), do you sit by silently?

o ii 2828

ṭu-⸢da⸣ pe-tu-ma lu-uṣ-bat ḫar-⸢ra-ni⸣

(o ii 28) "Open the way and let me set off on a campaign.

o ii 2929

d7.BI ⸢qar⸣-rad la ša₂-na-an lu-⸢up⸣-x x x

(o ii 29) "Let the Seven, unrivalled heroes, ...

o ii 3030

gišTUKUL-MEŠ-⸢ia⸣ ez-zu-tu šu-li-⸢ku i⸣-[da-a-a]

(o ii 30) "Make my furious weapons go at my side!

o ii 3131

u₃ at-⸢ta⸣ a-lik maḫ-ri-ia [a]-⸢lik⸣ ar₂-[ki-ia]

(o ii 31) "And you, be my advance guard, be my rear guard!"

o ii 3232

⸢di-šum⸣ an-ni-ta [...]

(o ii 32) [...] Išum [...] this,

o ii 3333

⸢i⸣-[pu-uš-ma] pa-a-še i-zak-kar [ana qu-ra]-du d⸢ER₃⸣.[RA]

(o ii 33) he opened his mouth to speak to the warrior Erra:

o ii 3434

⸢be⸣-lu₄ ⸢d⸣ER₃.RA min₃-su a-[na ...] ⸢ḪUL⸣ti₃ tak-⸢pu⸣-[ud]

(o ii 34) "Lord Erra, why do you plan evil against [...]?

o ii 3535

a-na sa-⸢pan⸣ [KUR].⸢KUR⸣ ḫul-lu-⸢uq⸣ [UN-MEŠ ḪUL]ti₃ tak-pu-ud-ma  ; ⸢la ta⸣-[tur ana] ⸢ar⸣-ki-ka

(o ii 35) "Without looking back, you are planning evil (namely) to flatten the lands (and) to destroy [the people]."

o ii 3636

dER₃.RA [pa-a]-⸢še⸣ i-[pu-uš-ma] i-qab-bi

(o ii 36) Erra opened his mouth to address

o ii 3737

a-na d⸢i-šum⸣ a-[lik] maḫ-ri-⸢šu₂⸣ [a-mat] i-zak-kar

(o ii 37) Išum, his advance guard. He spoke [the word]:

o ii 3838

di-⸢šum⸣ qu-⸢lam⸣-ma ši-[mi] qa-ba-a-a

(o ii 38) "Išum, pay attention to me and listen to what I say!

o ii 3939

aš₂-šu₂ ⸢UN-MEŠ⸣ da-ad₂-⸢me⸣ ša₂ taq-bu-u [ga]-⸢ma⸣-al-šin

(o ii 39) "Concerning the people of the inhabited world about whom you say: 'Save them!'

o ii 4040

a-⸢lik maḫ⸣-ri DINGIR-MEŠ ⸢en-qu d⸣i-šum ša₂ mi-⸢lik-šu₂⸣ dam-qu

(o ii 40) "Advance guard of the gods, wise Išum, whose advice is good:

o ii 4141

ina ⸢AN⸣e ri-ma-⸢ku⸣ ina KIti₃ ⸢lab⸣-ba-ka

(o ii 41) "I am the wild bull in heaven; I am the lion on earth;

o ii 4242

[ina] ⸢KUR⸣ šar-ra-⸢ku⸣ ina DINGIR-MEŠ ⸢ez⸣-ze-ka

(o ii 42) "I am king [in] the land; I am the most furious among the gods;

o ii 4343

[ina] ⸢d⸣i₂-gi₃-gi₃ qar-da-⸢ku⸣ ina dA.NUN.NA.KI gaš-rak

(o ii 43) "I am hero [among] the Igigi; I am the strongest among the Anunnaku;

o ii 4444

[ina] ⸢bu⸣-li₃ ma-ḫi-ṣa-⸢ku⸣ ina KURi šu-ba-ku

(o ii 44) "I am the hunter [among] livestock; I am the battering-ram into the mountain;

o ii 4545

ina ⸢a-pi⸣ dGIRA₂-[ku] ina qi₂-ši ma-ag!(AŠ-)ša₂-rak

(o ii 45) "[I am] fire in the reed-bed; I am the battleaxe in the forest;

o ii 4646

ina a-⸢lak⸣ ḫar-ra-nu u₂-ri-in-na-ku

(o ii 46) "I am the standard when going on campaign.

o ii 4747

ki-i ⸢TU₁₅⸣ a-za-qu ki-i dIŠKUR ⸢ur-ta⸣-ṣa-an

(o ii 47) "I blow like the wind; I rumble loudly like Adad;

o ii 4848

ki-i ⸢dUTU⸣ši a-bar-⸢ri kip⸣-pa-ta ⸢ka⸣-la-ma

(o ii 48) "Like the sun, I check the circumference of everything.

o ii 4949

a-na ⸢EDIN? uṣ?-ṣi?⸣-ma bi-⸢ib⸣-ba-ku

(o ii 49) "I am the wild sheep when I go out to open country.

o ii 5050

a-na ⸢na?⸣-me-e er-ru-ub-ma [ina] ⸢TUR₃?⸣ ra-⸢ma-ku?⸣ šub-ta

(o ii 50) "I enter the deserted land and take up residence [in] the animal stall.

o ii 5151

DINGIR-⸢MEŠ⸣ nap-ḫar-šu₂-nu ṣal-⸢ta?⸣ šaḫ-tu

(o ii 51) "All the gods fear a fight

o ii 5252

u₃ UN-MEŠ ṣal-mat ⸢SAG⸣.DU le-⸢qu⸣-[u₂] ši-ṭu-tu₄

(o ii 52) "and the black-headed people scorn (me).

o ii 5353

a-na-ku aš₂-šu₂ la iš!(AŠ₂-)ḫu-ṭu-ma zik-ri

(o ii 53) "As for me - because they do not fear my name

o ii 5454

⸢u₃⸣ ša₂ NUN dAMAR.UTU a-mat-su id-du-[ma] ⸢ip⸣-pu-uš ki-i lib-bu-uš

(o ii 54) "and disobey prince Marduk's word, he may act according to his heart -

o ii 5555

NUN dAMAR.UTU u₂-šag-gag-ma ina šub-⸢ti⸣-[šu₂ a]-de-ki-ma UN-MEŠ a-sa-pan

(o ii 55) "I will make prince Marduk furious, remove (him) from his dwelling and flatten the people."

o ii 5656

qu-ra-du dER₃.RA ana ŠU.AN.⸢NA IRI⸣ LUGAL DINGIR-MEŠ iš-ta-kan pa-ni-šu₂

(o ii 56) The warrior Erra set his face towards Šuanna (Babylon), the city of the king of the gods (Marduk),

o ii 5757

ana E₂.SAG.IL₂ E₂.GAL ANe u ⸢KI⸣[ti₃] i-ru-um-ma it-ta-ziz pa-nu-uš-šu₂

(o ii 57) He entered Esaggil, palace of heaven and earth, and stood before him.

o ii 5858

i-pu-uš-ma pa-a-še <a-na> ⸢LUGAL DINGIR⸣-MEŠ i-ta-mi

(o ii 58) He opened his mouth to speak to the king of the gods:

o ii 5959

min₃-su šu-kut-ti si-mat be-lu-ti-⸢ka⸣ [ša₂ GIM] MUL-MEŠ ⸢ša₂⸣-ma-[mi] lu-la-a ma-la-at  ; ⸢le⸣-qa-⸢a?-ti?⸣ u₂-ru-ši1

(o ii 59) "Why is the šukuttu-adornment, symbol of your lordship, which was full of splendour like the heavenly stars, taken with dirt ?

Reverse
Column i
r i 1r i 1

a-ge-⸢e⸣ be₂-lu-ti-ka [ša₂] ⸢GIM⸣ E₂.TE.ME.EN.AN.KI u₂-nam-ma-⸢ri⸣  ; E₂.ḪAL.AN.⸢KI⸣ pa-nu-šu kat₃-⸢mu⸣

(r i 1) "(Why) is the face of the crown of your lordship, [which] made Ehalanki bright like Etemenanki, covered?"

r i 22

i-pu-⸢uš⸣-ma pa-a-šu ⸢LUGAL⸣ DINGIR-MEŠ i-ta-a-mi

(r i 2) The king of the gods opened his mouth to speak

r i 33

a-na ⸢d⸣ER₃.RA qar-rad DINGIR-MEŠ a-mat i-zak-kar

(r i 3) to Erra, hero of the gods, and said:

r i 44

qu-ra-⸢du⸣ dER₃.RA aš₂-šu₂ šip-⸢ri⸣ ša₂-a-šu₂ ša₂ taq-bu-u₂ e-pe-šu₂

(r i 4) "Warrior Erra, concerning that work that you recommend to do:

r i 55

ul-tu ⸢ul⸣-lu a-gu-gu-ma ina šub-⸢ti⸣-ia at-bu-ma aš₂-ku-na a-bu-bu

(r i 5) "Long ago, I did become furious, left my dwelling and brought about the flood.

r i 66

ina šub-⸢ti⸣-ia at-bi-ma ši-piṭ ⸢AN⸣e u KIti₃ up-ta-aṭ-ṭir

(r i 6) "When I left my dwelling, the judgement of heaven and earth was dispelled;

r i 77

⸢AN⸣[e] ša₂ uš-tar-i-bu ša₂ MUL-<MEŠ> ⸢ša₂-ma-mi man-za⸣-as-su-nu iš-ni-ma  ; ul i-⸢tur aš₂⸣-ru-šu₂-un

(r i 7) "The heavens were made to tremble, such that the positions of the heavenly stars changed and did not return to their place;

r i 88

er-kal-⸢lu₄⸣ ša₂ i-nu-šu₂ ša₂ ši-ir-⸢ʾi⸣ bi-lat-⸢su⸣ im-ṭi-ma a-di ul-la  ; a-na ⸢e⸣-me-da aš₂-ṭa

(r i 8) "The underworld rocked (in such a way) that the yield of the furrow diminished and became forever difficult to rely on.

r i 99

ši-piṭ ⸢AN⸣e u KIti₃ ša₂ ⸢up⸣-ta-ṭi-ru IDIM im-⸢ta⸣-ṭi-ma me-lu it-taḫ-su  ; ⸢a-tur⸣ a-mur-ma a-na pe-te-⸢e⸣ im-⸢tar-ṣa⸣

(r i 9) "The judgement of heaven and earth was dispelled (in such a way) that underground water diminished and high water receded. (When) I turned to look at (it), it had become difficult to undo (it).

r i 1010

ša₂ ⸢šik⸣-na-at ⸢ZI⸣ti₃ nab-⸢nit⸣-si-na iṣ-ḫir-ma ul ⸢i-tur aš₂⸣-ru-šu₂-un

(r i 10) "The creations of living beings depleted and did not return to their place,

r i 1111

a-di ki-i lu₂ENGAR NUMUN-ši-na ⸢aṣ?⸣-bat ⸢ina⸣ qa-ti-ia

(r i 11) "until, like a farmer, I could hold (all) their seed in my hand.

r i 1212

E₂ e-pu-⸢uš⸣-ma u₂-šib ina lib₃-bi

(r i 12) "I made a house and sat inside.

r i 1313

šu-kut-tu ša₂ ina a-⸢bu-bi⸣ ud-da-ʾi-pu-u₂-ma i-ki-lu ši-kin-šu₂

(r i 13) "(As to) the appearance of the šukuttu-adornment, which was pushed away in the flood and had become dark:

r i 1414

ana šu-un-bu-uṭ zi-⸢mi⸣-ia u ub-bu-ub ṣu-ba-⸢ti⸣-ia dGIRA₂ um-ta-ʾi-ir

(r i 14) "I commanded the Fire-god to make my countenance shine and to cleanse my garments.

r i 1515

ul-tu šu-kut-⸢ti⸣ u₂-nam-me-ru-ma u₂-qat-tu-u₂ šip-ri

(r i 15) "After he made the šukuttu-adornment bright and finished the work for me,

r i 1616

a-ge-e be-⸢lu⸣-ti-ia an-na-ad-qu-ma a-⸢na⸣ aš₂-ri-ia a-tu-ru

(r i 16) "I dressed in the crown of my lordship and returned to my place.

r i 1717

zi-mu-⸢u₂⸣-a tub-bu-u₂-ma ga-lit ni-iṭ-li

(r i 17) "My appearance was haughty and my look fearsome!

r i 1818

UN-MEŠ ša₂ ina [a-bu]-bi i-si-ta-ma e-mu-ra e-peš šip-ri

(r i 18) "The people who were left behind in the flood, saw the accomplishment of the work.

r i 1919

gišTUKUL-MEŠ-⸢ia⸣ u₂-šat-ba-ma u₂-ḫal-⸢laq⸣ re-e-ḫa

(r i 19) "Should I (now) set my weapons in motion and destroy the rest?

r i 2020

um-ma-a-⸢ni⸣ šu-nu-ti a-na ABZU u₂-še-rid-ma e-la-šu₂-nu ul aq-⸢bi⸣

(r i 20) "I made those craftsmen descend to the Apsu and did not command them to go up.

r i 2121

ša₂ giš⸢MES el⸣-me-ši a-šar-šu-un u₂-⸢nak⸣-[kir]-⸢ma ul u₂-kal-lim⸣ mam-ma

(r i 21) "I changed the place of the mēsu-tree and amber and did not show anybody.

r i 2222

[en-na] ⸢aš₂⸣-šu₂ šip-ri ša₂-a-šu₂ ša₂ taq-⸢bu⸣-u₂ qu-ra-du dER₃.RA

(r i 22) "[Now then], warrior Erra, concerning that work that you recommend:

r i 2323

[a-li] ⸢gišMES⸣ UZU DINGIR-MEŠ ⸢si⸣-mat LUGAL gi-mir

(r i 23) "[Where] is the mēsu-tree, flesh of the gods, symbol of the king of the universe?

r i 2424

[iṣ-ṣu el]-⸢lu⸣ GURUŠ ṣi-ri ⸢ša₂⸣ šu-lu-ku a-na be-lu-ti

(r i 24) "The pure [tree], exalted young man,4 which is appropriate to lordship,

r i 2525

[ša₂ ina tam-ti₃] ⸢ra-pa-aš₂-ti A⸣-MEŠ 1 ME DANNA i-šid-su ik-šu-⸢du?⸣  ; [šu]-⸢pul?⸣ a-ra-al-[le-e]

(r i 25) "The roots of [which] reached the depths of the Underworld, 1 hundred bēru [into the] wide [sea].

r i 2626

[qim-mat-su ina e]-⸢la⸣-a-ti [em]-de-ti ANe ša₂ d[a-nu₃]

(r i 26) "[Its crest] is in contact with the heaven of [Anu] above.

r i 2727

[a-li eb-bu] ⸢na₄⸣ZA.GIN₃.DURU₅ ša₂ ⸢u₂-šam⸣-sa-ku [...]

(r i 27) "[Where is] the [bright] zagindurû-stone which I removed [...]?

r i 2828

[a-li d]NIN.ILDUM₂ lu₂⸢NAGAR⸣.[GAL] ⸢d⸣a-nu-ti-ia

(r i 28) "[Where is] Ninildum, the master joiner of my divinity,

r i 2929

[... d]⸢UTU⸣ši eb-bi mu-de-[e? ... ša₂]-a-šu₂ ša₂ GIM ⸢u₄⸣-[me ...]

(r i 29) "[...] pure [...] of Šamaš, who knows that [...], who [...] like the day,

r i 3030

[...] ina šap-lu-u₂-⸢a⸣ [...]

(r i 30) "[...] beneath me [...].

r i 3131

[a]-⸢li⸣ dKU₃.SI₂₂.BAN₃.DA ⸢ba⸣-[an ...]

(r i 31) "Where is Kusibanda, creator of [...]?

r i 3232

[a]-⸢li⸣ dNIN.<A₂>.GAL na-aš₂ [...]

(r i 32) "Where is Ninagal, bearer of [...],

r i 3333

ša₂ ⸢dan⸣-na ⸢URUDU⸣-ḪI.A GIM [...]

(r i 33) "who [...] hard copper like [...]?

r i 3434

[a]-⸢li⸣ NA₄-MEŠ na-as₂-qu-⸢ti⸣ [...]

(r i 34) "Where are the precious stones [...]?

r i 3535

[a-li] 7 ABGAL ap-⸢si⸣ [...]  ; ⸢ša₂⸣ ki-ma d⸢e₂⸣-[a ...]  ; šuk-lu-lu ⸢mu⸣-[ub-bi-bu ...]

(r i 35) "[Where are] the 7 sages of the Apsu [...], who are perfect [...] like Ea, cleansers [...]?"

r i 3636

[iš]-⸢mi⸣-šu-ma [... qu-ra-du dER₃.RA]

(r i 36) [Warrior Erra] heard him and [...];

r i 3737

[i]-⸢pu⸣-uš-ma pa-a-[še ...]

(r i 37) he opened his mouth [...]:

r i 3838

[...] x x x-ma [...]

(r i 38) "[...] ... and [...]

r i 3939

[...]-⸢ri?⸣-šu₂ ⸢E₁₁⸣ [...]

(r i 39) "I shall send down [...] his [...] ...

r i 4040

[...] x-ri-šu₂ ⸢E₁₁⸣ [...] x x [...]

(r i 40) "I shall send down [...] ... [...] his [...] ..."

r i 4141

[dAMAR.UTU ...] i-[na še-me]-šu

(r i 41) When [Marduk] heard [...],

r i 4242

[i-pu-uš-ma pa-a-še i-zak]-⸢kar⸣ a-⸢na⸣ [qu-ra-du] ⸢d⸣ER₃.RA

(r i 42) [he opened his mouth] to speak to [warrior] Erra:

r i 4343

[... ši]-⸢piṭ⸣ [ANe u KIti₃] ⸢up⸣-ta-aṭ-ṭar

(r i 43) "[...] the judgement of heaven and earth will become dispelled.

r i 4444

[... i-ba]-ʾu ⸢ma⸣-a-⸢tu⸣

(r i 44) "[...] will overtake the land.

r i 4545

[... da-um]-⸢ma⸣-ti x [...] x x

(r i 45) "[...] darkness ... [...] ...

r i 4646

[... i-te-eb-ba]-⸢am?-ma?⸣ MUL-MEŠ ša₂-⸢ma⸣-[mi ...]

(r i 46) "[... will arise] and the heavenly stars [...].

r i 4747

[... i]-⸢zi⸣-qam-ma ša₂ UN-MEŠ šik-na-at ⸢ZI⸣[ti₃ ...]

(r i 47) "[...] will blow and [...] of people, living beings [...].

r i 4848

[...] il-lu-nim-ma i-⸢ṣab⸣-bat [...]

(r i 48) "[...] will go up and [...] will seize [...].

r i 4949

[ša₂ qab]-li paṭ-ra-a-ti ma-⸢ḫir⸣-šu₂-⸢nu⸣ [...]

(r i 49) "[...] who confronts them with ungirt loincloth.

r i 5050

[dA.NUN].⸢NA⸣.KI il-lu-nim-ma šik-[na-at ZIti₃ ...]

(r i 50) "The Anunnaku will go up and [...] the living beings.

r i 5151

[a-di gišTUKUL]-MEŠ-ia la an-na-ad-qu [...]

(r i 51) "[As long as] I have not put on my weapons, [...]?"

r i 5252

[d]⸢ER₃⸣.RA an-ni-tu₂ [i-na še-me-šu]

(r i 52) [When] Erra [heard] this,

r i 5353

[i-pu]-⸢uš⸣-ma pa-a-še i-⸢zak⸣-[kar ...]

(r i 53) he opened his mouth to speak [...]:

Column ii
r ii 1r ii 1

NUN dAMAR.UTU a-⸢di at⸣-ta a-na E₂ [... te-ru-bu]-⸢ma⸣ dGIRA₂ TUG₂-ka  ; <<tu>>-⸢ub-ba⸣-bu ta-tur-⸢ru aš₂⸣-ruk-ka

(r ii 1) "Prince Marduk, while you enter into [...] house and the Fire-god cleanses your garment, and (until) you return to your place,

r ii 22

a-di ul-lu a-rad-di-ma ši-piṭ ⸢AN⸣[e KIti₃] ⸢u₂⸣-dan-na-an

(r ii 2) "until that time I will lead and reinforce the judgement of heaven and earth.

r ii 33

a-na ANe el!(IL-)li-ma a-na di₂-[gi₃-gi₃ ...] ur-ta

(r ii 3) "I will go up to heaven and [...] an order to the Igigi.

r ii 44

ur-ra-du a-na ABZU dA.NUN.NA.⸢KI u₂⸣-paq-qad

(r ii 4) "I will go down to Apsu (and) care for the Anunnaku.

r ii 55

gal-le-e šam-ru-ti a-na KUR.NU.GI₄.[A] a-ṭar-rad-ma

(r ii 5) "I will send off the furious gallû-demons to the Land Of No Return and

r ii 66

gišTUKUL-ia ez-zu-tu UGU-šu₂-nu ⸢uš⸣-za-za

(r ii 6) "make my furious weapons triumph against them.

r ii 77

ša TU₁₅ lem-nu ki-ma MUŠEN a-kas₂-sa-[a] ⸢i⸣-di-šu

(r ii 7) "I will bind the wings of the evil wind like a bird's.

r ii 88

a-na E₂ ša₂-a-šu₂ a-šar te-ru-bu [NUN] ⸢d⸣AMAR.UTU

(r ii 8) "At that house which you will enter, prince Marduk,

r ii 99

ZA₃ u 2.30 ša₂ KA₂-ka da-nu₃ ⸢d⸣EN.LIL₂ u₂-⸢šar⸣-[ba]-⸢ṣu⸣ GIM GU₄

(r ii 9) "on the right and on the left of your gate, I will make Anu and Ellil lie in wait like oxen."

r ii 1010

iš-mi-šu-ma NUN [d]⸢AMAR⸣.UTU

(r ii 10) Prince Marduk heard him and

r ii 1111

a-⸢mat⸣ dER₃.RA iq-bu-u₂ UGU-šu₂ ⸢i⸣-ṭib

(r ii 11) the word Erra said pleased him.

(catchline)
r ii 1212

it-bi-ma ina ⸢šub⸣-ti-šu₂ a-⸢šar la⸣ a-ri ana šu-bat dA.⸢NUN.NA.KI⸣  ; iš-ta-kan pa-[ni]-šu₂

(r ii 12) He got up from his dwelling, an inaccessible place, and set his face towards the dwelling of the Anunnaku.

(colophon)
3 empty lines; colophon spaced out
r ii 1313

⸢GIM⸣ SUMUN-BI ⸢ša₃⸣-ṭir-ma ba-⸢ri₃⸣

(r ii 13) Written and checked according to its original.

r ii 1414

ṭup-pi mdna-bi-um—šu-ma—GARun

(r ii 14) Tablet of Nabu-šum-iškun, son of Kandalanu, chief scribe of the field marshal. Whoever takes away this tablet

r ii 1515

⸢PEŠ.GAL⸣ mkan-dal₃-a-ni lu₂GAL A.⸢BA?⸣

r ii 1616

ša lu₂tur-ta-ni <ša> IR ⸢IM?⸣ UR₅tu₄

r ii 1717

⸢MU?⸣ x [...] x [...] x [...]

(r ii 17) ... [...] ... [...] ... [...]

r ii 1818

x [...] x [...]

(r ii 18) ... [...] ... [...]

r ii 1919

[...]

(r ii 19) [...]

r ii 2020

ša₂ x [...]

(r ii 20) of ... [...]

r ii 2121

itiŠE U₄ 7-KAM₂ lim-[mu ...]

(r ii 21) Addaru (month XII), 7th day, eponymate of [...].

1The reading of urūšu is uncertain.

1 Epithet of Išum ; it means "exalted sceptre".

2 Sumerian i-šum means "praised slaughterer".

3 Epithet of Erra ; it means "the lord who roams at night".

4 Wordplay on Akkadian mēsu (a type of tree ) - Sumerian MES "hero , young man".

Created by Greta Van Buylaere for the AHRC-funded GKAB Project, 2010 and released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/cams/gkab/P338333
